summary: Problem q1 asks to prove equivalence (mutual absolute continuity) of the Φ⁴₃ measure on 𝕋³ under a smooth deterministic shift T_ψ, a frontier quasi-invariance result of Cameron–Martin type. The current state shows no completed solution: the only solve attempt errored out interactively without producing a solution.tex, and the best-proof tracker reports 100 open issues, indicating no substantive verified progress. Solvability is assessed low (19%) reflecting the genuine difficulty of controlling the singular Φ⁴₃ interaction under shift.
problems: No solution.tex was ever produced — the interactive solve (claude-opus-4-8) terminated with an error, leaving zero usable proof text.

Core mathematical blocker: establishing absolute continuity requires controlling the change in the renormalized Φ⁴₃ energy (the ∫:φ⁴: and mass/renormalization counterterms) under the shift φ → φ+ψ, where the cross terms involve ill-defined products of the rough field with ψ that must be renormalized.

The Cameron–Martin / Girsanov-type density must be shown to be a genuine L¹ (or finite, positive a.s.) Radon–Nikodym derivative; integrability of exp of the shifted interaction is the key unverified estimate.

The 'Best Proof' tracker reporting 100 open issues suggests the proof skeleton is essentially empty — no lemma structure, no renormalization bookkeeping, no measure-theoretic equivalence argument exists.

No identification of which function space / regularity structure framework (paracontrolled vs. regularity structures vs. variational/stochastic quantization) is being used, leaving the proof strategy undefined.
highlights: The expected answer direction is known and correct (the measures ARE equivalent — a Cameron–Martin-type quasi-invariance result), giving a clear target to aim the proof at.

The shift ψ is smooth and nonzero, which is favorable: smoothness of ψ means the most singular cross-terms (rough × rough) are avoided, and the difficult products are rough-field × smooth-function type, which are more tractable.
The problem is on the compact torus 𝕋³, so there are no infinite-volume / large-scale issues to handle — only the UV (short-distance) renormalization matters.
The single tracked issue is already marked 'resolved' at the workflow level, so housekeeping is not the bottleneck — effort can focus entirely on mathematical content.

suggested_todos.title: Re-run the solver with a fixed pipeline so an actual solution.tex is produced; the prior attempt errored before emitting any output.

Fix the proof framework explicitly (recommend stochastic quantization / variational approach à la Barashkov–Gubinelli or Hairer–Mattingly) and write down the renormalized Φ⁴₃ energy functional as the formal density.
Compute and renormalize the energy difference V(φ+ψ) − V(φ), isolating the cross-terms ∫ :φ³: ψ, ∫ :φ²: ψ², etc., and identify exactly which require renormalization given smooth ψ.
Prove the key integrability/exponential-moment estimate establishing the Radon–Nikodym derivative is in L¹(μ) and strictly positive, yielding mutual absolute continuity.
Decompose the 100 tracked open issues into a structured lemma dependency graph so progress can be measured against a real proof skeleton.
Document the smoothness-of-ψ simplifications explicitly to bound scope and avoid attempting the harder rough-shift generalization.
